blocks.text: let himself be a person at the bonfire. He was mostly successful at it.
kept the Nadia detail in the background all evening. Not the center — just there.
didn't mention it again. But when Nadia sat down by the fire he noted where she positioned herself.
said nothing about it. He let the night be a night.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You made it! I was starting to think you found something in that rental that needed more cataloguing.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We made it. You were right about the fire — this is a good one.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I told everyone it would be. Cade said I oversold it. Dean — back me up.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: I said nothing.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Your silence was a vote against. I clocked it.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: Get close to the fire, it gets cold fast out here. Are you two staying for the whole weekend? Marcus keeps inviting you two into plans like you've been here for days.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Sunday afternoon. And we're happy to be included — this is already better than whatever we would have done otherwise.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: Marcus has been building up to this bonfire for two months. We've all been hearing about it.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I said it would be a good weekend. I stand by that.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: You said transformative. I have a text. I can show them the text.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: That word was used in a specific context.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: The context was: this weekend is going to be transformative.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: To be fair, the fire is very good.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Thank you, Dean.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: That's all I'm giving you.
Marcus laughed at himself like it cost him nothing. There was something genuinely easy about him — the kind of person who treats a small win the same as a big one and seems richer for it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: What's the Appalachian Trail story? I heard it mentioned and I feel like I need the full version.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Oh. Oh, this is a gift. Cade.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: We have new people. They deserve to know.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: It was one afternoon and everyone has taken an unreasonable amount of mileage from it.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: He had a map of the wrong trail. He walked the perimeter of a parking lot for eleven hours because his map said the trailhead was the summit.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I'm sorry — the parking lot?

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: It was a large lot. There was elevation variance.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: He apologized to a parking attendant. He thought the attendant was a ranger.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: He was very official-looking.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: He asked the parking attendant for the summit trail.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: He was helpful.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: What did the attendant charge for the summit?

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: Nothing. He validated my parking.

blocks.prompt: The whole group is laughing. This is the first real moment of the weekend. What is Julian doing?

options.label: Laughing. Actually laughing — not the polite kind.
Watching Cade tell the story against himself. That's a specific kind of ease.
Watching Mara laugh. He'd forgotten how long it had been since he'd seen that.
